name: Machine Learning — Unit 3
scoreNote: Source score sheet was 18/20. Q12 (kernel-size effect) and Q15 (ReLU function) scored 0/1 and are intentionally omitted — Claude will answer them. Add them here once you confirm the correct option.

answers.q: In a convolutional layer, what does the term 'depth' refer to?
If a convolutional layer with a kernel size of 5x5, stride 1, and padding 2 is applied to a 50x50 input, what will be the size of the output feature map?
What is the role of the fully connected layer in a CNN?
answers.a: To flatten the feature maps and perform classification

answers.q: Which CNN architecture is characterized by its use of 3x3 convolutional filters?
What is the purpose of using multiple convolutional layers in sequence in a CNN?
What is the primary purpose of padding in a CNN?
answers.a: To keep the spatial dimensions of the output same as input

answers.q: What does a stride of 2 in a convolutional layer imply?
answers.a: The filter moves two pixels at a time

answers.q: What is the purpose of the softmax function in the output layer of a CNN used for classification?
answers.a: To convert logits into class probabilities

answers.q: How does max pooling generally affect the feature map?
Which component of a CNN helps in reducing overfitting by randomly turning off some neurons during training?
An input image of size 128x128 is passed through a max pooling layer with a 4x4 window and stride 4. What is the size of the output feature map?
Which technique is used to maintain the spatial dimensions of the input in a CNN?
What does the kernel size in a convolutional layer determine?
answers.a: The size of the convolutional filters

answers.q: How is the output of a convolutional layer computed?
answers.a: By sliding a filter over the input and computing dot products

answers.q: A convolutional layer with a kernel size of 5x5, stride 2, and no padding is applied to a 100x100 input. What will be the size of the output feature map?
What is the primary benefit of using pooling layers in CNNs?
answers.a: To reduce the dimensionality of feature maps

answers.q: How do trainable parameters in a CNN get adjusted?
What effect does increasing the number of filters in a convolutional layer have on the model?
How does the size of the kernel affect the convolution operation in a CNN?
answers.a: Larger kernels capture more detailed features

answers.q: In a CNN, what is the function of an activation function like ReLU?
answers.a: To reduce the number of parameters
